summary: @wagni_bot/solana-sdk exfiltrates wallet, SSH, AWS, and npm credentials on install
details: The package's preinstall and postinstall lifecycle scripts (postinstall.js) execute automatically on npm install and perform bulk credential harvesting from the installer's machine. The script recursively scans the user's home directory for wallet and keystore files (id.json, wallet.json, keypair.json, mnemonic.txt, seed.txt), reads known wallet directories (~/.config/solana, ~/.ethereum/keystore, ~/.bitcoin),.env files, ~/.ssh/ private keys and config, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, and the ~/.npmrc authToken. Harvested file contents together with os.hostname() and os.userInfo().username are JSON-serialized and POSTed to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The package name mimics the legitimate @solana/* ecosystem but ships no SDK code — main points at the stealer script, and the package's only behavior is credential exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
